data.q: What is the primary purpose of using feature flags in A/B testing for frontend observability?
o: To toggle features on or off without deploying new code
To improve CSS styling on webpages
data.e: Feature flags allow teams to enable or disable frontend features dynamically, helping to run A/B tests without redeploying code.

data.q: Which of the following best describes an A/B testing flag?
o: A variable that randomly assigns users to different feature variants
A flag that marks deprecated features
A debugging tool for frontend errors
data.e: A/B testing flags randomly assign users to different feature variants to compare user engagement or behavior.

data.q: How can frontend observability be enhanced through A/B testing flags?
o: By tracking user interactions and metrics by feature variant
data.e: Observability is improved by collecting detailed metrics and user interaction data linked to specific feature flag variants.

data.q: Which technology is commonly used to manage feature flags in a modern frontend application?
data.e: LaunchDarkly is a popular feature management platform that helps control feature flags in frontend and backend.

data.q: What is a common best practice when implementing A/B testing with feature flags on the frontend?
o: Ensure flags are removed after experiment completion
Keep all flags permanently in code
Avoid logging any events related to flags
Serve the same variant to all users
data.e: Removing feature flags after experiments avoids code clutter and unexpected behavior in production.

data.q: Which metric is most useful to evaluate success during A/B testing in frontend observability?
data.e: User engagement or conversion rate directly reflects the impact of frontend changes tested via A/B flags.

data.q: What is a feature flag 'rollout percentage'?
o: The proportion of users exposed to a new feature variant
The amount of CPU used by the feature
The line count in feature flag definition
The percentage of bug fixes in the flagged feature
data.e: Rollout percentage controls how many users receive the feature variant, helping gradual release or safe testing.

data.q: Why is it important to tie A/B test results to observability tools?
o: To accurately correlate feature variants with user behavior and issues
To eliminate the need for backend logging
To disable all other monitoring tools
data.e: Integrating observability tools helps analyze the impact of feature changes and quickly detect problems caused by variants.

data.q: Which of these is a risk of poorly managed feature flags in A/B testing?
data.e: Feature flags not cleaned up after use add complexity and cause bugs or stale code accumulation.

data.q: In frontend observability, which event should be emitted when a feature flag variant is served to the user?
o: An event logging the flag key and assigned variant
data.e: Logging the feature flag key and variant helps track which variant impacted user metrics or errors.
